raw_consumer_statement:
A fraudulent Booking.com hotel reservation in the amount of {$530.00} was placed on my Barclays account for a stay at the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX in Virginia on XX/XX/year>. 

This transaction was part of a broader identity theft incident involving attempted fraudulent account openings and misuse of my personal information, including my email address. During the same timeframe, I was receiving numerous fraudulent account-creation texts and identity-theft related activity involving my personal information. 

I reported this transaction to Barclays immediately while the fraudulent activity was actively occurring. 

Because the fraudster had access to my email account information, I received XXXX reservation emails despite never authorizing the transaction. 

Importantly, subsequent communications related to the reservation were addressed not to me, but to an individual identified as XXXX XXXX, which is not my name and is further evidence that the reservation was created and used by a third party during the broader identity theft incident. 

I have never stayed at this hotel, had no reason to be in XXXX, Virginia, and was not physically present there on the date of the alleged stay. I did not authorize, participate in, or benefit from the transaction in any manner. 

Barclays initially issued a provisional credit but later reversed it after claiming the merchant provided documentation validating the charge. However, Barclays has not identified any competent evidence demonstrating that I personally authorized or used the reservation. 

To my knowledge, Barclays has not provided : any signed hotel registration card ; evidence of identification presented at check-in ; proof I occupied the room ; device/IP information tying me to the booking ; or any other meaningful evidence establishing authorization. 

The mere existence of a reservation confirmation email does not establish authorization, especially where the fraudster had access to my email address as part of the broader identity theft event. 

Additionally, any merchant records reflecting use by XXXX XXXX would further confirm that the transaction was not authorized by me. 

I am requesting that Barclays : Reopen and properly reinvestigate the dispute ; Provide all documentation relied upon in denying the claim ; Reverse the charge and restore the disputed credit. 

I believe Barclays failed to conduct a reasonable investigation into this unauthorized transaction.
